summary: Audience tape with good clarity captures a memorable Help‑Slip‑Fire sequence and a strong second set, despite first‑set pitch quirks.
review: The recording is an audience source that delivers clear sound for the standout Help > Slip > Fire segment and the energetic second half of the show. While the first set suffers from a half‑step high pitch anomaly and some keyboard work (Vince) draws criticism, the crowd’s excitement and highlights like Bird Song make it a compelling listen.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the performance—especially the Help‑Slip‑Fire combo and second‑set energy—and note decent recording clarity; only a few points of criticism (keyboard, pitch) keep the rating just above four.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear capture with strong presence on Help‑Slip‑Fire; first‑set sounds a half‑step high in pitch, likely tape‑speed issue, but second set returns to true pitch.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Fire On The Mountain
show_quality.setlist_flow: The second set shines with high crowd energy and tight transitions, while the first set is marred by pitch problems and uneven keyboard work.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His playing on Stagger Lee sounds off and wailing at the end is a bit out of tune, though overall he recovers later in the set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince's keyboard work is criticized as pedestrian and boring, especially during Fire; piano was louder than usual with less delicacy.
